items.title: Neuquén Province to pitch first US bond since 2017
items.description: Neuquén province will pitch foreign investors in New York on its first new international bond in nearly a decade as the local government reaps the rewards of a shale oil boom. Leer más

items.title: JD Vance says wife Usha and son 'happy and healthy' after birth of fourth child
items.description: The couple welcomed their fourth child, Alec Neel Vance, on Sunday morning.

items.title: Jubilation in Spain as La Roja beat Argentina to win second World Cup title
items.description: Fans in Spanish city on Mediterranean cheer on both sides as Spain defeat Argentina 1-0 in extra time.

items.title: Senegal: Ex-president Sall arrives back for meeting with successor
items.description: Senegal's ex-president Macky Sall, who is campaigning to become the next UN secretary-general, arrived in Dakar Friday for a brief meeting with his successor, a visit criticised by the former leader's detractors.

items.title: Merz and Macron: A Franco-German week of harmony
items.description: Friedrich Merz and Emmanuel Macron did everything they could this week to demonstrate a close partnership. This is likely because both fear that the next French president could be nationalist Marine Le Pen.

items.title: Who is Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s new prime minister?
items.description: On Monday, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham succeeds Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Burnham's politics have been shaped by England's north-south class divide.

items.title: Monaco assassination plot takes a new turn
items.description: A woman suspected of plotting to assassinate a Ukrainian-Cypriot businessman in Monaco has been found dead in Ukraine. Two men with ties to Ukraine's intelligence services have now been brought into custody.

items.title: 'Good night, sweet dreams': Derryn Hinch farewelled at state funeral
items.description: Broadcaster Ray Martin, Australian actress Jacki Weaver and performer Rhonda Burchmore are among the entertainment heavyweights to pay tribute to Derryn Hinch at his state funeral service.

items.title: Algeria battles intense wildfires amid heatwave
items.description: Algeria continues to battle intense wildfires amid high temperatures, with authorities saying they have mobilised significant resources to fight them.

items.title: Private jets flock to Montana
items.description: Rents in Bozeman, Montana have skyrocketed as wealthy out-of-towners flock to the city to experience the American West.
items.source: but locals can't afford the trailer park

items.title: Why a 90-year-old treaty limits NATO in the Black Sea
items.description: A 90-year-old treaty still shapes the balance of power in the Black Sea. As Ukraine fights on against Russia, can NATO project power into waters it can't enter freely?
